[[JA:太部古天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444530}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tabekoten Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tabe koten shrine.jpg|250px|Tabekoten Shrine]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}} Nakakawabe, Aza Tenjin 1-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Tenjin|Q1305727|WD=Q1305727|lt=Kotenjin|1=Tenjin}}　{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}　{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=gold/silver-hei shrine|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=silver-hei shrine|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=Second Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tabekoten
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tabekoten Shrine''' (たべこてんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Tabe Shrine, a shikinaisha of Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. Another theory identifies Tabe Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tabe Shrine|Q11444532|WD=Q11444532|1=Tabe Shrine}} in the same town. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. The annual festival on the second Saturday and Sunday of April is called the Tabekoten Shrine Festival, in which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are pulled. === Overview === The founding date is unknown. It is said to have formerly been called Tabe Shrine and Tabekotenjin, and later to have merged Sugawara no Michizane. In the Edo period, it was said to be the village guardian shrine and general shrine of Kawabe for Kawabe village, Ishigami village, Kashio village, and Tochii village (now the west-bank area of the {{ill|lt=Hida River|Q3366160|WD=Q3366160|1=Hida River}} in Kawabe). Since Meiji, it has been the village guardian shrine of Nakakawabe and Nishitochii. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Tenjin|Q1305727|WD=Q1305727|lt=Kotenjin|1=Tenjin}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} === Precinct shrines === *Nagano-sha *Gozu-sha *Jadoku-sha *Shirahige-sha === Tabekoten Shrine Festival === *A festival transmitted since the Edo period. Two floats are pulled from Tochii Shrine to Tabekoten Shrine, and lion dance is dedicated in the precincts. *At 6:00 a.m. on Sunday, the “sake-buying rite” is held. Early in the morning, a parishioner wearing the mask of “Haiō,” messenger of Tenjin, goes with the lion to a sake brewery and buys sake to offer to Tenjin. *This rite is performed entirely as {{ill|lt=pantomime|Q39818|WD=Q39818|1=Mime}}. *Haiō is said either to be {{ill|lt=Gaozong of Tang|Q9703|WD=Q9703|1=Emperor Gaozong of Tang}} of {{ill|lt=Han|Q7209|WD=Q7209|1=Han dynasty}} or another name of a Han warrior, but details are unknown. Haiō appears wearing a mask (close to a {{ill|lt=tengu|Q742360|WD=Q742360|1=Tengu}} mask but with a less prominent nose) wrapped with a shimenawa-like band. This resembles the fly-chasing man in the annual festival of {{ill|lt=Afushina Shrine|Q11657364|WD=Q11657364|1=Abushina Shrine}}, also in Kawabe, said to represent {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Cultural properties === *Sake-buying rite (town-designated intangible folk cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://demo-kawabe.wikii.jp/wp-content/uploads/2013/02/町指定文化財一覧.pdf |title=List of town-designated cultural properties |format=PDF |publisher=Kawabe Town |accessdate=2013-04-22}} === Transportation === *5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tabekoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tabekoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:川辺町の建築物]] [[Category:川辺町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]][[Category:qq]]

[[JA:太部古天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11444530}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Tabekoten Shrine | 画像=[[File:Tabe koten shrine.jpg|250px|Tabekoten Shrine]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}} Nakakawabe, Aza Tenjin 1-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Tenjin|Q1305727|WD=Q1305727|lt=Kotenjin|1=Tenjin}}　{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}　{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|lt=gold/silver-hei shrine|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=silver-hei shrine|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿= | 例祭=Second Sunday of April | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tabekoten
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Tabekoten Shrine''' (たべこてんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kawabe|Q1203748|WD=Q1203748|1=Kawabe, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Tabe Shrine, a shikinaisha of Kamo District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. Another theory identifies Tabe Shrine with {{ill|lt=Tabe Shrine|Q11444532|WD=Q11444532|1=Tabe Shrine}} in the same town. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. The annual festival on the second Saturday and Sunday of April is called the Tabekoten Shrine Festival, in which two {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|WD=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are pulled. === Overview === The founding date is unknown. It is said to have formerly been called Tabe Shrine and Tabekotenjin, and later to have merged Sugawara no Michizane. In the Edo period, it was said to be the village guardian shrine and general shrine of Kawabe for Kawabe village, Ishigami village, Kashio village, and Tochii village (now the west-bank area of the {{ill|lt=Hida River|Q3366160|WD=Q3366160|1=Hida River}} in Kawabe). Since Meiji, it has been the village guardian shrine of Nakakawabe and Nishitochii. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Tenjin|Q1305727|WD=Q1305727|lt=Kotenjin|1=Tenjin}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} === Precinct shrines === *Nagano-sha *Gozu-sha *Jadoku-sha *Shirahige-sha === Tabekoten Shrine Festival === *A festival transmitted since the Edo period. Two floats are pulled from Tochii Shrine to Tabekoten Shrine, and lion dance is dedicated in the precincts. *At 6:00 a.m. on Sunday, the “sake-buying rite” is held. Early in the morning, a parishioner wearing the mask of “Haiō,” messenger of Tenjin, goes with the lion to a sake brewery and buys sake to offer to Tenjin. *This rite is performed entirely as {{ill|lt=pantomime|Q39818|WD=Q39818|1=Mime}}. *Haiō is said either to be {{ill|lt=Gaozong of Tang|Q9703|WD=Q9703|1=Emperor Gaozong of Tang}} of {{ill|lt=Han|Q7209|WD=Q7209|1=Han dynasty}} or another name of a Han warrior, but details are unknown. Haiō appears wearing a mask (close to a {{ill|lt=tengu|Q742360|WD=Q742360|1=Tengu}} mask but with a less prominent nose) wrapped with a shimenawa-like band. This resembles the fly-chasing man in the annual festival of {{ill|lt=Afushina Shrine|Q11657364|WD=Q11657364|1=Abushina Shrine}}, also in Kawabe, said to represent {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Cultural properties === *Sake-buying rite (town-designated intangible folk cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://demo-kawabe.wikii.jp/wp-content/uploads/2013/02/町指定文化財一覧.pdf |title=List of town-designated cultural properties |format=PDF |publisher=Kawabe Town |accessdate=2013-04-22}} === Transportation === *5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tabekoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tabekoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:川辺町の建築物]] [[Category:川辺町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]]

Early in the morning, a parishioner wearing the mask of “Haiō,” messenger of Tenjin, goes with the lion to a sake brewery and buys sake to offer to Tenjin. *This rite is performed entirely as {{ill|lt=pantomime|Q39818|WD=Q39818|1=Mime}}. *Haiō is said either to be {{ill|lt=Gaozong of Tang|Q9703|WD=Q9703|1=Emperor Gaozong of Tang}} of {{ill|lt=Han|Q7209|WD=Q7209|1=Han dynasty}} or another name of a Han warrior, but details are unknown. Haiō appears wearing a mask (close to a {{ill|lt=tengu|Q742360|WD=Q742360|1=Tengu}} mask but with a less prominent nose) wrapped with a shimenawa-like band. This resembles the fly-chasing man in the annual festival of {{ill|lt=Afushina Shrine|Q11657364|WD=Q11657364|1=Abushina Shrine}}, also in Kawabe, said to represent {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. === Cultural properties === *Sake-buying rite (town-designated intangible folk cultural property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://demo-kawabe.wikii.jp/wp-content/uploads/2013/02/町指定文化財一覧.pdf |title=List of town-designated cultural properties |format=PDF |publisher=Kawabe Town |accessdate=2013-04-22}} === Transportation === *5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nakakawabe Station|Q5358732|WD=Q5358732|1=Nakakawabe Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tabekoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tabekoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:川辺町の建築物]] [[Category:川辺町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]]
